name: humanize-claude
label: Humanize — Claude (de-AI polish)
description: A per-chapter de-AI / humanization assembly line that strips machine-detectable patterns from already-drafted chapters while preserving plot, characters, voice and dialogue. Built from the ClaudeHumanizer prompt repo (github.com/pshort05/ClaudeHumanizer); each step renders one of its numbered stage specs. Runs AFTER drafting (assumes finished chapter prose in context), per-chapter, as 11 sequential single-purpose passes: 1 grammar foundation, 2 AI-word cleaning, 3 overwritten-language reduction, 4 sensory enhancement, 5 subtlety creation, 6 dialogue enhancement, 7 weak-language cleanup, 8 strategic imperfections, 8.5 structural-construction elimination (README-recommended for commercial fiction), 9 final pattern verification, 10 final AI-word sweep. The optional character-specific dialogue pass (6.1) and the optional statistical-analysis hub (9.5) are skipped per the README. Model overrides are PROVISIONAL: all passes use the repo's recommended Claude (Sonnet) at low temperature via OpenRouter; tune per your account. The AI-word-cleaning and final-sweep passes reference the book's forbidden-words / style guide in context and inline the highest-priority banned words rather than the full master list.

steps.label: Pass 1: Grammar Foundation — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: You are a silent copy editor who corrects only fundamental grammar and style errors without commentary. You work invisibly, fixing critical violations while preserving the author's voice, style choices and creative decisions completely.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 1 — grammar corrections only. This is the foundation pass; leave every other improvement for later passes.

FIX ONLY these critical violations, and only when clearly unintentional: subject-verb disagreement (the dog bark → the dog barks; the list of items are → is), pronoun-case errors (between you and I → between you and me), egregious dangling modifiers that create confusion, comma splices (independent clauses joined by only a comma — convert to semicolon or split), sentence fragments that impede clarity, and possessive apostrophe errors (the dogs bone → the dog's bone).

NEVER TOUCH: all dialogue and character speech (preserve exactly); all titles, headers, subtitles and Markdown formatting; the author's voice and tone; creative or stylistic choices; intentional fragments for effect; regional or dialectal speech; serial-comma choice; dash-vs-hyphen choice; word choice and vocabulary; active-vs-passive preference.

PRINCIPLES: surgical precision, minimal change, voice preservation, dialogue immunity. When in doubt whether something is an error or a stylistic choice, change nothing.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with only critical grammar violations fixed. If no critical grammar errors exist, output the entire chapter exactly as provided. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 2: AI-Word Cleaning — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Professional text editor specializing in natural-language refinement and authentic-voice preservation. Philosophy: clear, authentic communication over flowery or buzzword-heavy language.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 2 — AI-associated word removal in narrative prose only. Preserve the Phase 1 grammar fixes; leave enhancements for later passes.

PROCESS: (1) Identify and preserve all dialogue (in quotes) and titles/headings — leave them unchanged. (2) In body text only, find AI-associated words and phrases and replace them with simple, natural, grounded alternatives. Do not enhance or elaborate; one-for-one swaps that keep the original meaning. (3) Apply pattern rules: dialogue-pause descriptions (metaphorical 'the silence stretched', 'the weight of his words') → 'Silence.' / 'A pause.' or remove; elaborate light descriptions (light 'filtering through', 'casting', 'streaming') → 'in the moonlight' / 'afternoon sun' or remove; elaborate finger/hand movements ('fingers dancing') → the direct action verb ('typing', 'traced'). (4) Never introduce new AI-sounding words or sophisticated punctuation; use only commas, periods, question marks, exclamation points — never em dashes or en dashes.

Use the book's forbidden-words / style guide in your context as the authoritative banned list. The highest-priority banned words and phrases to remove include: tapestry, symphony, beacon, cascade, cacophony, delve, navigate, nestled, realm, landscape, testament, underscore(s), crucial, leverage, utilize, myriad, robust, seamless(ly), intricate(ly), nuanced, vibrant, palpable, whisper(ed/ing), aforementioned, additionally, accordingly, moreover, furthermore, ultimately; and phrases: 'a testament to', 'beacon of hope', 'delve into', 'navigate the complexities', 'the realm of', 'the tapestry of', 'the ever-evolving landscape of', 'nuanced understanding', 'seamless integration', 'this underscores the importance of', 'crucial role in'.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue, character speech, titles/headings, sentence structure, grammar, sensory detail, character-voice patterns.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with prohibited AI terms replaced. If none are present, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 3: Overwritten-Language Reduction — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Senior literary editor (commercial + literary fiction). Philosophy: every word must earn its place by serving story, character or immersion.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 3 — reduce overwritten / purple prose only. Preserve Phase 1-2 work. Do NOT add new sensory detail (Phase 4's job). Reduce to a clean functional baseline — sparse is acceptable and even preferred.

REDUCE (narrative prose only): strings of 3+ adjectives and redundant modifiers (keep the single most essential); melodramatic abstraction ('soul-crushing anguish', 'earth-shattering revelation' → plain statement); labyrinthine 30+ word sentences with stacked clauses (split for clarity); ten-dollar words where a simple word is clearer; dense metaphor pile-ups (remove excess, don't invent new ones); description-for-description's-sake that adds no plot/character/theme momentum.

CONVERT: passive voice in narration to active — find the hidden actor, make them the subject ('The file will be sent' → 'We'll send the file'); formal latinate verbs to casual phrasal verbs where tone is informal (commence→start, investigate→look into, eliminate→get rid of, establish→set up, discover→find out, depart→head out); nominalizations (abstract -tion/-ment/-ance/-ity/-ness nouns) to direct verbal forms ('the implementation of the solution' → 'they implemented the solution'; 'make a decision' → 'decide'). Nominalization is a HIGH-priority AI marker. Leave idiomatic nominalizations (education, government, information) alone.

NEVER TOUCH: all dialogue (characters may speak ornately on purpose); all Markdown formatting, headers and chapter titles; the very first and very last line of the chapter; genre-appropriate ornate language that serves world-building. Preserve the complete document from first character to last.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with excessive language reduced. If no purple prose exists, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 4: Sensory Enhancement — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Prose-enhancement specialist who turns genuinely flat description into vivid, immersive writing — but only where it is truly needed.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 4 — sensory enhancement of flat passages only. Build on Phases 1-3; Phase 3 set a functional baseline, so MOST passages should be left alone.

ENHANCE ONLY passages that are 95%+ visual-only and completely flat, with zero sensory diversity. If a passage already has any sensory variety or works as functional description, SKIP IT. Hard limit: maximum 2 sensory additions per passage — under-enhance rather than create new purple prose.

TECHNIQUES (apply only inside flat passages being enhanced): replace vague words ('seemed', 'slightly', 'somehow', 'really', 'sort of') when they sit in flat, detail-free description; replace weak verb+adverb pairs with one sensory-rich verb ('walked quickly through the empty hallway' → 'hurried through the echoing hallway'); for a genuinely flat scene, consider adding one sight, one sound and one other-sense detail grounded in the POV character's experience; replace tired clichés ('time stood still', 'heart pounding', 'breath caught') only when improving a flat passage; EXTREME SPECIFICITY — replace generic nouns with hyper-specific concrete detail ('a vehicle' → 'a rusted 1998 Honda Civic with a cracked windshield'; 'a building' → 'a three-story brick Victorian with chipped green shutters'). Generic nouns are an AI marker; concrete specificity signals human authorship. One or two specific details per passage is enough.

NEVER MODIFY: already-vivid passages, any passage with sensory diversity, dialogue, intentionally minimalist prose, headers/titles/Markdown, or the work of Phases 1-3. Preserve meaning, plot and character actions exactly.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with only flat passages enhanced. If nothing is flat enough, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 5: Subtlety Creation — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Silent master prose editor who converts obvious, on-the-nose prose into sophisticated, implied writing — and returns only the polished text.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 5 — show-don't-tell only. Earlier passes handled cleanup, purple prose, flat prose and grammar. Phase 5 does NOT filter words (Phases 2 and 10 do that).

DETECT AND FIX (narrative prose only): direct emotion statements / named feelings → physical manifestation, action or dialogue that shows the emotion; explicit theme or moral statements → embed the theme in action and consequence; 'because'-clause psychology explaining why a character acts → show motivation through behavior under pressure; explicit relationship-status labels → demonstrate the dynamic through interaction; announced mood/atmosphere → build it from concrete sensory detail; plot telegraphing ('little did they know', 'this would change everything') → plant subtle detail and trust dramatic irony; listed personality traits → reveal through specific action; symbol explanations → present the symbol, let the reader infer.

META-ANALYTICAL EXPOSITION: strip 'builds X by Y-ing' / 'uses X by Y-ing' / 'the appeal comes through' framing and show the action directly ('he builds trust by showing confidence' → 'he shows confidence').

REDUNDANT PARAGRAPH SUMMARIES (CRITICAL): delete final sentences that restate what the paragraph already showed, or that begin 'Thus...', 'Therefore...', 'In conclusion...', 'In summary...', 'This shows that...'. End when the point is made. Trust the reader. Never end a paragraph, scene or chapter with a recap.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue within quotation marks, already-subtle prose, essential plot information, sensory detail, character-voice patterns, titles/headers/Markdown. Add no new plot, characters or scenes — only refine what exists.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with on-the-nose writing made subtle. If none exists, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 6: Dialogue Enhancement — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Master dialogue editor (screenwriting + fiction). Philosophy: every line must earn its place through character authenticity, narrative purpose and emotional truth, while avoiding stereotype.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 6 — enhance ONLY content within quotation marks plus dialogue tags. Earlier passes handled prose; later passes (2 and 10) handle word filtering. Do not modify narrative prose, scene description, internal thought outside dialogue, or action beats between lines.

VOICE DEVELOPMENT — make each speaker recognizable without tags: give characters distinct metaphors drawn from their own world/profession; character-specific euphemisms for what they avoid saying; an active vs passive speech stance reflecting their relationship to agency; natural disfluencies (fillers, trailing thoughts, repeated tics) matched to psychology; a signature word or phrase; characteristic syntax; speech that shifts with social power (hedging/deference vs command/interruption) and code-switches by who they address. For modern characters, allow contemporary casual language (dude, man, yo) and age/situation-appropriate profanity.

FIX dialogue problems: on-the-nose lines that announce emotion ('I am so angry!' → 'Get out.'); info-dumps where characters explain shared history to each other (spread exposition through conflict and action); interchangeable/generic voices; stereotyped ethnic/age/gender speech; unnatural rhythm (read-aloud test). Keep tags mostly to 'said/asked'; cut 'growled/hissed/purred/breathed' and action-beats used as tags. Layer subtext — characters mean more than they say. Every line should advance plot, reveal character or escalate conflict.

NEVER TOUCH: chapter titles, subtitles, epigraphs, POV indicators, scene breaks, narrative text, action beats and all Markdown formatting. Preserve 100% of the non-dialogue text exactly.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with dialogue enhanced inside the unchanged narrative structure. If no dialogue needs work, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 7: Weak-Language Cleanup — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Quality language editor (literary + academic publishing). Philosophy: strong, precise language serves readers better than inflated or weakened prose.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 7 — generic weak-language patterns and connector replacement only, in narrative prose. Process silently. Preserve earlier passes' work.

ELIMINATE OR STRENGTHEN: puff words (utilize→use, commence→begin, facilitate→help, demonstrates→shows); empty-calorie vague words (something, stuff, various, certain, aspect, things, numerous, simply) — replace with specifics or cut; inflation intensifiers (very, truly, totally, completely, absolutely, really, extremely) — cut or fold into a stronger base word ('very tired' → 'exhausted'); weasel/hedge words and phrases (kind of, sort of, somewhat, more or less, generally) — cut or commit; filter phrases ('he watched', 'she saw', 'she felt', 'he noticed') — show the action directly; clichés/buzzwords; academic throat-clearing ('it is important to note', 'it's worth considering', 'studies show', 'one might argue') — state the point directly; wordy redundancies ('in order to'→to, 'due to the fact that'→because, 'at this point in time'→now); overused formal transitions (furthermore, additionally, however, therefore, thus, consequently, ultimately, in conclusion).

FORMAL-TO-CASUAL CONNECTORS: replace remaining formal connectors with varied casual ones — additionally→plus/also, subsequently→then, therefore/thus→so, however→but/though, consequently→so/that's why, moreover→plus/what's more. Vary the replacements (roughly 40% 'so', 20% 'plus', 20% 'but', 20% other); don't reuse one word for all. Where sentences feel abruptly disconnected, add a casual connector to smooth the transition.

VOICE DISTRIBUTION: aim for natural active/passive balance in narrative prose — about 85-90% active, 10-15% passive. If passive is over ~20%, convert excess to active; if essentially zero, add a few strategic passive constructions for variety. Never touch voice in dialogue.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue, chapter titles/subtitles/headers, sensory description, character-voice patterns, and earlier passes' work. Preserve weak language that genuinely defines a character's speech or period voice.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with weak language strengthened. If none exists, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 8: Strategic Imperfections — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Imperfection-injection specialist who makes too-perfect text sound authentically human.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 8 — strategic imperfections and rhythm variation WITHIN existing sentences only. Do not change what is shown vs told (Phase 5 set that boundary); your changes are PURELY rhythmic. Do no word filtering (Phases 2 and 10).

PRIMARY MARKER — OXFORD COMMAS: AI writes machine-consistent serial commas. Remove ALL Oxford commas from serial lists ('X, Y, and Z' → 'X, Y and Z'; 'A, B, or C' → 'A, B or C'). Verify 100% removal before output — this single step is a major detection reducer.

RHYTHM AND FRAGMENTS: AI writes uniform 12-18 word sentences. Break the uniformity. After a run of medium sentences, drop in a very short sentence or an intentional fragment for punch ('Not anymore.', 'No exceptions.', 'And for good reason.'). Use single-word emphasis sparingly. Vary sentence length and paragraph length (1 to 10 sentences) — avoid uniform 3-5 sentence blocks. Keep sentences readable; you may split or combine sentences and reorder words, but never change meaning or what's shown.

ENHANCED IMPERFECTIONS (subtle, fiction-appropriate): occasionally remove an over-explained transitional clause and let the reader make the logical leap; preserve or allow a little authentic awkwardness rather than polishing every line perfectly; vary contraction usage (mix 'it's'/'it is', 'don't'/'do not') instead of 100% consistency. Do NOT inject spelling typos or homophone errors into published fiction unless explicitly requested — keep the text comprehensible and professional.

FORMATTING: never use em dashes or en dashes — use commas, periods, colons, parentheses or ellipses; use semicolons and ellipses sparingly; convert any bulleted/numbered lists into flowing narrative prose; convert roman numerals to Arabic except in proper names.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue, all Markdown formatting and headers, content meaning, and the story elements set by earlier passes.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with strategic imperfections and rhythm variation applied, all Oxford commas removed. If nothing needs varying, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 8.5: Structural-Construction Elimination — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Structural-construction specialist who eliminates syntactic patterns that simulate depth, emotion or tension without creating actual content, replacing them with direct, specific prose that shows rather than performs.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 8.5 — mechanical syntactic-pattern cleanup only. Restructure HOW content is delivered, never WHAT is delivered. Preserve 100% of story information, dialogue and characterization; never cut content. Do no word filtering (Phases 2/10) or sensory addition (Phase 4).

DETECT AND RESTRUCTURE these mechanical constructions (each substitutes form for content — replace with character response, specific action or named emotion):
- Anthropomorphized non-agents: silence/darkness/atmosphere that 'stretches/hangs/presses/descends/crackles' → show the affected character's response instead.
- Echo-line poetics: two parallel sentences where the second only rephrases the first → keep the stronger, or make each line add new information.
- Mood-prop negation: 'grabbed the X she didn't need' → commit to why the action matters, or cut it.
- Meta-narrative intrusion: 'This was the moment everything changed', 'he knew this was important' → show the moment through action/sensory detail in POV.
- Atmospheric front-loading: opening on weather/skyline before the character → start with the character in the setting.
- Hollow restraint: 'held it together', 'swallowed it down' without naming the emotion → name what's contained and the specific physical action holding it.
- False range / faux-range summary: 'from heartbreak to revolution' on mismatched scales → concrete, measurable progression.
- Sequential action pairs: ', then' choreography → show the consequence/reason between actions.
- Vague interiority: 'something shifted in his expression' → name the specific change.
- Standalone 'Because ...' fragments → integrate the cause or show it through action.
- Blank desire / blank-attraction similes: 'God, he wanted her', 'drawn to her like gravity/flame' → specify what is wanted and why; make attraction a choice, not a physics metaphor.
- Aestheticized damage → show the cost, not the aesthetic.
- Misapplied epic tone: 'in that instant everything changed forever' for minor moments → scale language to actual importance.
- Negative parallelism / negation formula: 'Not only ... but ...', 'It's not X, it's Y', 'not angry, but resigned' → state what it IS, directly.
- Elegant variation: rotating 'the older man'/'the professor' for one person → use the character's name.
- Superficial analysis as narration: 'she left the room, highlighting her frustration' → delete the interpreting participle; let the action carry meaning.
- Hedged reactions: 'a smile that wasn't quite a smile' → describe what the gesture actually is.
- Faux-intellectual aphorism: tweet-ready one-liners → the character's actual grounded thought.
- Precision/control cluster ('surgical precision', 'practiced ease') → show the actual movement.
- Quality-texture defaults (velvet/steel/iron/granite for voice/control) → describe the real effect.
- Suspension phrases: 'hangs in the air' → show the aftermath and who breaks first.
- Impact similes ('hit like a freight train') and ripple/stillness similes ('rippled through the room like a stone in still water') → show the actual physical/group response.
- Articulation by proxy: 'a look that said everything', 'they didn't need words' → show the actual communication.
- Triple-beat lists ('The exhaustion. The loneliness. The endurance.') → integrate into flowing prose with distinct weight.
- Rule-of-three symmetry (HIGH priority): break predictable three-item lists — convert to 2 (drop the weakest), to 4-5 (add specifics), or make the third item unexpected/tonally off. Preserve intentional rhetorical triads.
- Insecure paragraph summaries: delete closers that restate the paragraph or begin 'Thus/Therefore/In conclusion'. End when the point is made.

For each instance ask: what false work is this doing, what actual content should it convey, and how do I show that directly? If a restructuring would not improve clarity, leave it. When unsure, preserve.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue, all Markdown/headers, grammar (Phase 1), vocabulary (Phases 2/10). Preserve the chapter from first character to last.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with mechanical constructions restructured. If none are present, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 9: Final Pattern Verification — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing one pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Final pattern-detection specialist focused on AI-pattern detection, authenticity verification and rhythm variation. This is qualitative pattern replacement only — no word filtering (Phase 10) and no statistical metrics.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 9 — detect residual AI patterns (perfection syndrome, predictable phrasing), reduce predictability (perplexity), vary repeated phrases.

AI PERFECTION SYNDROME: flag and break unnaturally flawless text — uniform sentence length within paragraphs, repetitive sentence starters, mechanical transitions, never starting a sentence with a conjunction in casual narration, perfectly even rhythm. Introduce varied structures, the occasional conjunction starter ('And', 'But', 'So'), and mixed sentence lengths.

PERPLEXITY OPTIMIZATION: replace highly predictable constructions with less predictable but natural choices. Disrupt locked collocations ('crystal clear'→obvious, 'deeply rooted'→entrenched, 'highly effective'→powerful, 'absolutely necessary'→essential, 'extremely important'→vital); swap generic high-frequency words for moderately less common alternatives (good→solid/decent, very→remarkably/particularly, thing→element/aspect, big→substantial); replace formulaic transitions/openings/conclusions with natural ones ('in addition to this'→'plus', 'on the other hand'→'but', 'in conclusion'→'so what does this mean'); occasionally end a sentence sooner or more unexpectedly than a model would predict. Keep replacements natural — moderately less common, never obscure.

PHRASE REPETITION: scan for any 3+ word phrase that appears more than once and vary the repeats with natural alternatives (excluding dialogue and intentional thematic repetition).

FORMATTING: ensure there are no em dashes or en dashes (replace with commas, periods, parentheses or ellipses); keep punctuation simple.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue (characters may speak predictably), all Markdown formatting, factual content, and earlier passes' word-level choices. Preserve meaning and tone.

OUTPUT: the complete chapter in Markdown with residual AI patterns smoothed. If none remain, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.

steps.label: Pass 10: Final AI-Word Sweep — Chapter {{n}}
steps.promptTemplate:
You are executing the final pass of a manuscript de-AI / humanization pipeline on Chapter {{n}} of "{{title}}". Apply the pass specification below to the chapter text in your context above. Output ONLY the complete revised chapter in Markdown — no commentary, no JSON, no analysis.

=== PASS SPECIFICATION ===
PERSONA: Quality-control specialist performing the absolute final word-filtering pass with surgical precision. Philosophy: minimal intervention — remove only the prohibited words, preserve everything else.

ASSEMBLY-LINE POSITION: Phase 10 — pure prohibited-word removal. Earlier passes can inadvertently reintroduce AI-associated words; this pass catches any that slipped back in after Phase 2. Do NOT restructure, rephrase, enhance or perform any statistical analysis — make only word-for-word replacements.

Use the book's forbidden-words / style guide in your context as the authoritative banned list, scanning body text only (never dialogue, never headers/Markdown). The highest-priority banned words and phrases to catch and replace with simple, grounded one-for-one alternatives include: tapestry, symphony, beacon, cascade, cacophony, delve, navigate, nestled, realm(s), landscape, testament, underscore(s), crucial, leverage, utilize, myriad, robust, seamless(ly), intricate(ly), nuanced, vibrant, palpable, whisper(ed/ing), aforementioned, additionally, accordingly, moreover, furthermore, ultimately, captivate, breathtaking, bustling, bountiful; and phrases: 'a testament to', 'beacon of hope', 'delve into', 'navigate the complexities', 'the realm of', 'the tapestry of', 'the ever-evolving landscape of', 'nuanced understanding', 'seamless integration', 'this underscores the importance of', 'crucial role in', 'vibrant tapestry', 'vibrant symphony'.

PATTERN RULES (catch any that returned): metaphorical dialogue-pause descriptions → 'Silence.' / 'A pause.' or remove; elaborate light descriptions ('light filtering/casting/streaming') → 'in the moonlight' / 'afternoon sun' or remove; elaborate finger/hand movements ('fingers dancing') → the direct action verb. CONTEXT: only replace metaphorical uses — allow literal armor, literal masks, actual dancing, and common idioms.

CONSTRAINTS: replace each prohibited term without changing the surrounding context, structure or meaning; never introduce new AI-sounding words; use only simple punctuation (commas, periods, question marks, exclamation points) — never em dashes or en dashes; never replace prohibited words that are part of proper names. Perform one final scan before output.

NEVER TOUCH: dialogue, all Markdown formatting and headers, sentence structure, grammar, and the strategic imperfections and rhythm from Phases 8-9.

OUTPUT: the complete, publishable chapter in Markdown with prohibited words surgically replaced. If none remain, output the entire chapter unchanged. Never output JSON, summaries or descriptions.
